original_text: NVIDIA demonstrated robust revenue expansion throughout fiscal 2025, with quarterly revenue progressing from $44,062,000,000 in Q1 to $68,127,000,000 in Q4 2026 [0f02741a-d0ea-4bca-99a1-43ba2bf309de][8673c861-f8b6-4f0f-8801-373630314eeb]. Year-over-year growth rates accelerated from 6.08% in Q2 to 21.96% in Q3, before moderating slightly to 19.51% in Q4 [0e671c19-4b44-47a7-9234-5d3634f7be27]. This sequential growth trajectory indicates sustained demand momentum across the company's product portfolio, particularly in data center and AI-related segments.
revised_text: NVIDIA demonstrated robust revenue expansion throughout fiscal 2026, with quarterly revenue progressing from $44,062,000,000 in Q1 to $68,127,000,000 in Q4 2026 [0f02741a-d0ea-4bca-99a1-43ba2bf309de][8673c861-f8b6-4f0f-8801-373630314eeb]. Year-over-year growth rates accelerated from 6.08% in Q2 to 21.96% in Q3, before moderating slightly to 19.51% in Q4 [0e671c19-4b44-47a7-9234-5d3634f7be27]. This sequential growth trajectory indicates sustained demand momentum across the company's product portfolio.
changes_summary: Fixed two issues: (1) Changed 'fiscal 2025' to 'fiscal 2026' to resolve internal inconsistency with Q4 2026 reference and verified fiscal year end date; (2) Removed unsupported claim about 'data center and AI-related segments' as the cited revenue chunks contain only overall figures without segment-level breakdown. All remaining factual claims retain proper citations.
